institution.source_quote: Program: Cybersecurity Certificate (CAS.CYBE) - Trident Technical College

program_focus.value: Information Assurance, Cybersecurity, Systems Security, Infrastructure Security, Digital Forensics, Network Security
program_focus.source_quote: It is designed to provide expertise in information assurance and cybersecurity to prepare you for employment in the fast-growing field of cybersecurity...Students will gain the knowledge and skills to develop and implement systems and infrastructure security in business and industry.

program_focus_details.value: This certificate program is designed for individuals with experience or training in systems and network operations. It is designed to provide expertise in information assurance and cybersecurity to prepare you for employment in the fast-growing field of cybersecurity. This program is ideal if you are employed or are pursuing employment in a business that includes a cybersecurity workforce as part of the organization. Students will gain the knowledge and skills to develop and implement systems and infrastructure security in business and industry.
program_focus_details.source_quote: This certificate program is designed for individuals with experience or training in systems and network operations. It is designed to provide expertise in information assurance and cybersecurity to prepare you for employment in the fast-growing field of cybersecurity. This program is ideal if you are employed or are pursuing employment in a business that includes a cybersecurity workforce as part of the organization. Students will gain the knowledge and skills to develop and implement systems and infrastructure security in business and industry.

prerequisites.value: Admission into this program requires proof of high school graduation (or GED) and qualifying scores on SAT, ACT or the TTC placement test.
prerequisites.source_quote: Admission into this program requires proof of high school graduation (or GED) and qualifying scores on SAT, ACT or the TTC placement test.

num_courses.source_quote:
Major Requirements

CPT 282 - Information Systems Security Credit: 3
CYB 269 - Digital Forensics Credit: 3
CYB 285 - Cybersecurity Capstone Credit: 3
IST 165 - Implementing and Administering Windows Directory Services Credit: 3
IST 166 - Network Fundamentals Credit: 3
IST 190 - Linux Essentials Credit: 3
IST 191 - Linux System Administration Credit: 3
IST 291 - Fundamentals of Network Security I Credit: 3
IST 293 - IT and Data Assurance I Credit: 3
IST 294 - IT and Data Assurance II Credit: 3

cost_per_class_long.source_quote:
Out of State Residency
Hours: 1, Tuition*: $378.64

total_cost.source_quote: In-state (Charleston/Berkeley/Dorchester County Residency): 30 credit hours * $200/credit hour + (2 * $18.34 registration fee) = $6036.68. Out-of-state: 30 credit hours * $378.64/credit hour + (2 * $18.34 registration fee) = $11395.88

program_managed_by.source_quote: Trident Technical College's Center for Cybersecurity provides students and the community with cybersecurity resources as well as academic and professional development opportunities.

nsa_cae_cd_accredited.value: Yes, Trident Technical College has been designated as a National Center of Academic Excellence (CAE) in Cyber Defense Education (CDE).
nsa_cae_cd_accredited.source_quote: Trident Technical College has been designated as a National Center of Academic Excellence (CAE) in Cyber Defense Education (CDE). This designation was awarded by the National Security Agency (NSA) and Department of Homeland Security (DHS) upon meeting their stringent requirements for cybersecurity curriculum and program criteria.

cert_prep.value: This program will help you prepare for CompTIA Linux+, Security +, EC-Council Certified Ethical Hacking and other certification exams.
cert_prep.source_quote: This program will help you prepare for CompTIA Linux+, Security +, EC-Council Certified Ethical Hacking and other certification exams.

num_fulltime_faculty.source_quote: Terry Richburg, Frank W. Gibbes, Chris Griffith, Curtis Morris, Luke Batchelor

finance_options.value: Students can apply for financial aid by completing the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) and submitting it to TTC's school code (004920).
finance_options.source_quote: Students can apply for financial aid by completing the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) and submitting it to TTC's school code (004920).
